product_overview: Web chess training application for deliberate move selection with candidate notes, engine feedback, AI reflection, challenge-mode reasoning gate, and replayable learning analytics.

general_info.name: Disciplined Chess Training Session
general_info.overview: Deliver structured training sessions where users draft candidates, commit one move, and learn from engine-grounded AI reflection over time.

goal_and_context.business_goal: Increase user learning outcomes and repeat training sessions by making move decisions explicit and reviewable.
goal_and_context.user_problem: Players often commit moves impulsively and only see post-hoc engine scores without clear reasoning feedback.

actors_and_roles.system_service: Session API + Engine worker + Reflection worker

business_rules.description: Only legal moves can be saved or committed for the active position.
Candidate drafts do not mutate the official board state before commit.
Challenge mode requires sufficient reasoning or a follow-up answer before commit when feature flag is enabled.
If reflection service is unavailable, commit still succeeds with fallback reflection status.

acceptance_criteria.user_story: As a player I want to start or resume a training session quickly.

bdd.given: A signed-in player opens the training application.
and_preconditions: Configured engine level is within allowed bounds
bdd.when: The player starts a session with mode and engine level or reconnects to an existing one.
bdd.then: The system returns active session state with current turn and board position.
and_postconditions: Current turn can be resumed after reconnect

acceptance_criteria.user_story: As a player I want to manage candidate moves and notes before commit.

bdd.given: An active turn exists with a legal board position.
and_preconditions: Client has the current turn version
bdd.when: The player creates or updates draft candidates and notes.
bdd.then: Draft candidates are stored and turn version is incremented without changing committed board state.

acceptance_criteria.user_story: As a player I want post-commit reflection grounded in engine analysis.

bdd.given: A player has selected a legal move for the active turn.
and_preconditions: Engine analysis service is reachable or fallback is available
Commit request contains active turn version
bdd.when: The player commits the selected move.
bdd.then: The move is committed, engine response is applied, and reflection is stored with tags.

acceptance_criteria.user_story: As a player in challenge mode I want reasoning quality checks before commit.

and_preconditions: Challenge feature flag can be toggled
bdd.when: The player commits with weak reasoning and no follow-up answer.
bdd.then: Commit is blocked with challenge prompt until sufficient answer is provided or flag is disabled.
and_postconditions: User can proceed after valid follow-up
Fail-open behavior applies if reflection service is unavailable

acceptance_criteria.user_story: As a player or coach I want replayable history and stable learning metrics.

bdd.given: At least one committed turn exists in session history.
and_preconditions: Metrics aggregation can query by date range
bdd.when: The user requests history or metrics.
bdd.then: System returns paginated replay data and aggregate metrics including reflection distributions.
and_postconditions: Historical records stay queryable across model-version changes
Trend metrics are reproducible for same filters

non_functional_requirements.description: Local move validation responsiveness target under 200 ms.
Durable session log survives refresh and reconnect.
Candidate note updates are non-blocking for board interaction.
Draft-save failures are retryable without losing turn context.
Reflection latency target at or below 3 seconds in standard mode.
Reflection output is bounded and safe to render.
Challenge mode controlled by feature flag kill switch.
Commit flow fails open with clear fallback when reflection service is unavailable.
History supports paginated incremental loading for long sessions.
Metrics remain stable and queryable across reflection model version changes.

test_cases.assertion: Session and first turn are returned
State remains draft and version increments
Next turn created and FEN updated
Reflection tags are list-like and persisted

test_cases.action: Commit with weak reasoning and no answer

test_cases.assertion: Rendered text strips tags and is bounded
Effective timeout is capped to 3 seconds
Returns eval and source or INVALID_FEN error
Turn restored and next undo fails with NOTHING_TO_UNDO

test_cases.setup: Web app local storage has session id
test_cases.assertion: Frontend resumes active turn and candidates
Autosave is non-blocking and retry path exists
